pressure_assessment.reason: Run succeeds but result surface is partial; design result persistence before patching

lane_1_r142_evidence_reconciliation.correction_note: R142 JSON had model_api_called=false in lane_4 but model_api_called=true in safety_constraints. Reconciled to model_api_called=true based on logs showing 2 MiniMax API calls.

lane_2_runstore_model.note: update_run_completion(token_counts, last_ai_message, first_human_message, error) exists — but only stores token usage, not full result
lane_2_runstore_model.patch_surface: RunRecord dataclass — add result/output/messages fields; worker.py — capture final channel_values and write to record

lane_3_http_run_response_model.patch_surface: RunResponse model + _record_to_response() — add result field; wait_run endpoint or get_run endpoint to surface stored result

lane_4_runevent_model.patch_surface: RunEventStore is already canonical log; no model change needed — existing events can be projected for final result

lane_5_streambridge_model.values_mode_data: serialize_channel_values(channel_values) — dict with messages, title, artifacts, todos
lane_5_streambridge_model.false_negative_reason: R142 inspection used isinstance(ev_data, str) — but data is a dict (serialized channel_values), not a string. model_response_detected was false negative.
lane_5_streambridge_model.persistence_candidate: limited — StreamBridge contains full channel_values in values mode, but events are only available during active stream
lane_5_streambridge_model.patch_surface: StreamBridge not suitable as primary result store — ephemeral per-run

capture_points.available_data: checkpointer has final channel_values; but worker doesn't read from checkpointer here
capture_points.risk: medium — requires checkpointer read at end of worker
capture_points.reason: checkpointer read in worker adds coupling

capture_points.available_data: journal has buffered events; get_completion_data() returns token counts + last_ai_message
capture_points.risk: low — journal already writes to RunEventStore; minimal extension
capture_points.reason: journal already captures final AI message; just need to also capture full result dict

capture_points.risk: low — extension of existing update_run_completion call
capture_points.reason: update_run_completion already called with similar data; just expand fields captured

capture_points.location: POST /runs/wait — checkpointer read after run completes
capture_points.risk: low — no code change needed in worker
capture_points.reason: wait endpoint already reads checkpointer successfully

lane_6_worker_capture_points.recommended_capture_point: worker.py finally block — journal.get_completion_data() → update_run_completion() — expand to capture serialize_channel_values(final_channel_values) as result

lane_7_caller_expectations.client_stream_mechanism: stream() generator yields StreamEvent(type, data) until end events
lane_7_caller_expectations.client_chat_mechanism: chat() accumulates delta chunks, returns last AI message text
lane_7_caller_expectations.frontend_expects: useStream hook receives state.values (channel_values dict) via onFinish callback
lane_7_caller_expectations.frontend_result_access: via SSE stream onFinish, not via GET /runs/{run_id}
lane_7_caller_expectations.current_gap: No persistent result accessible via GET /runs/{run_id} — only metadata; result only available via SSE stream or POST /runs/wait

design_options.description: Add result/output/messages fields to RunRecord dataclass. Worker captures final channel_values and writes to record via update_run_completion-like mechanism.

design_options.backwards_compatibility: high — no new fields, just new query
design_options.frontend_usefulness: medium — requires GET /runs/{run_id}/events or new endpoint
design_options.description: Use existing RunEventStore as canonical log. GET /runs/{run_id} projects final result from stored events (llm.ai.response events). No new storage needed.
No result persistence. HTTP GET returns metadata only. Caller must use SSE stream or POST /wait. Not acceptable for caller UX.

design_options.option: D — Hybrid (RunEventStore canonical + RunStore latest_result summary)
design_options.frontend_usefulness: high — GET /runs/{run_id} returns latest_result; full history in events
design_options.description: RunEventStore keeps full event log. RunStore record gains latest_result summary (last AI message text, message count). GET /runs/{run_id} returns latest_result snapshot.

lane_8_design_options.recommended_design: Option A (RunStore Snapshot) — simplest path to giving GET /runs/{run_id} a result field. Expand update_run_completion to capture serialize_channel_values final state as result field on RunRecord.
lane_8_design_options.reason: Option A directly addresses the R142 finding (RunStore record has no result field). minimal changes to worker (capture final state in finally block via journal or checkpointer read). GET /runs/{run_id} immediately becomes more useful. Option D is better long-term but Option A is sufficient for now.

r142c_patch_package.expected_diff_summary: 1. RunRecord.add result field (dict); 2. update_run_completion gains result dict param; 3. worker.py finally block calls update_run_completion with serialize_channel_values final state; 4. RunResponse.add result field; 5. _record_to_response copies record.result to response
validation_plan: R142C inline smoke test: GET /runs/{run_id} has result field with non-empty messages
r142c_patch_package.rollback_plan: Revert RunRecord result field addition; revert update_run_completion change; revert RunResponse change — all are additive only

updates.description: Option A recommended — RunRecord gains result field, worker captures final state via journal.get_completion_data() + serialize_channel_values
Recommended: worker.py finally block — journal.get_completion_data() already called; expand to capture serialize_channel_values(final_state) as result
RunEventStore already stores llm.ai.response events with full AIMessage content as dict — already a canonical log
DeerFlowClient uses stream, not result polling. Frontend uses useStream hook onFinish(state.values). Result not read from GET /runs/{run_id} currently.
R142 model_response_detected=False was false negative — StreamEvent.data is a dict (serialize_channel_values output), not a string. Detection worked but isinstance check was wrong.
